name: Self-Attention
summary: Self-attention is a mechanism where each element in a sequence computes attention weights over all other elements in the same sequence, allowing the model to capture dependencies regardless of distance. Unlike cross-attention (which attends to a different sequence), self-attention operates within a single sequence. In the Transformer, self-attention uses queries, keys, and values derived from the same input, computing scaled dot-product attention scores that determine how much each position attends to every other position. Multi-head self-attention runs multiple attention functions in parallel, allowing the model to attend to different types of relationships simultaneously.
query_hooks: How does self-attention differ from attention?
What are queries, keys, and values?
